item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Noooooooooo!" *dives sideways protecting the Most Expensive Machine with his body*

blat blat BLAT blat blat BLAT blat

"Owwww."

"Josh, that's a Scranton reality anchor. Anomalous paintball pellets couldn't have touched it anyway."

"Laaaaaame. And anyway we could totally get real paintball pellets if we felt like it."

"I think technically they're called markers." blat "Aurgh! Right in the bruise. Why!?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More like STF amirite?

item.title: Re: Last note
item.content:encoded: Don't change it. I was so wrapped up in the narrative by that point, I was skimming past the timestamps and most of the repeating sentences by that point in my haste to see what happened next, so I would have missed it otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As subtle as a paintball in the OW! MY EYE! CHRIST! Why would anybody DO that!?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disagree, if only because I was so engrossed in the narrative that I was skipping the timestamps by that point. :)

item.title: Re: Greendale Human Beings!
item.content:encoded:
You know, I've been wondering about that myself lately.

I guess since that there aren't many cute nicknames you can give to a group of paintball-focused commandos, they must have been called something really generic. When "Community" came out they must have jumped at the chance of a proper nickname.

item.title: Greendale Human Beings!
item.content:encoded: Since this skip predates the sitcom Community by over 20 years, I'm curious as to what MTF-Iota-17 was called before then.

item.title: Amazing.
item.content:encoded:
This is how you make a light-hearted skip, that still has serious need for containment.

Also, the thought of site-19 being trashed because a MTF suck at paintball is just too funny to not upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A completely nonlethal Keter, and it works. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kasrzyszak is the real name of a friend of a friend (who is Polish). I liked the sound of it so I asked him if I could use it in a skip. He said it was fine.

Dr. Ben Kasrzyszak also appeared in SCP-2258. I just really like using that name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Google seems to suggest "Kasrzyszak" is indeed fake, but "Kasprzyszak" seems to be very real. Could just be an error.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's fun and well written but I just cannot upvote it because od how ridiculous the polish researchers name sounds. I'm polish and believe me, Kasrzyszak would be a tongue breaker even for me. It's made up, isn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You've got an amnesiac-amnestic mix-up in the first collapsible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this was one of the first SCPs I read over while searching under the latest high-rated SCPs page. The concept of a paintball arena with active players being more intrusive with the Foundation sold me. Actually I already liked it before reading the logs where they "fought" against MTFs, but reading those timestamped logs added more tension than I initially expected. Good one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dammit, dammit, I completely forgot to thank the people who helped me workshop this. Thanks to BananaRepublic, Ian is Me, Mistbourne and jhh665.

item.title: Re: Last note
item.content:encoded: I certainly wasn't paying attention to the decreased time between breaches, and the note was helpful in I'm calling attention to that and explaining it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I just about realized that now that I've read the article. I feel silly, I thought this was going to be melodramatic with "give your life to the foundation for greater good" and whatnot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Paintball doesn't really kill anyone though, a Foundation researcher is probably willing to deal with some paintball bruises for a few days instead of risk a billion dollar equipment failure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All delicate equipment must immediately be stored away, or in worst-case scenarios protected with staff member's bodies.

This stood out to me in the bad way. You can replace equipment, but replacing people is not feasible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a tale in that somewhere…

"So, hey, ever heard of this alternate reality called the 'lolfoundation'?"

<much time passes>

"HOLY SHIT THEY BEAT ABLE AND HIS CYBORG VELOCIRAPTORS BY LEARNING THAUMATURGY, IT'S TIME TO GIVE 682 A PAINTBALL GUN"

<more time passes>

"And that was how Dr. Bright discovered that, among other things, making an entire team of himselves from Navy Seals was probably the best idea he'd had since bacon ice cream."

item.title: Re: Last note
item.content:encoded:
I agree. For realistic article purposes, it makes sense. Except for maybe that could be part of the briefing when you get assigned.

Storytelling wise, however? It adds something for the less interested in the article and takes something from those that are more invested (at least from my point of view). Depends how you want to handle that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a naming convention. There are actually a number of MTFs tied to particular SCPs or sites.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would the team handling containment really be an MTF? If they're tethered to one specific facility - one specific skip! - they're not really mobile, are they?

I suppose it could be for paygrade reasons… with such an absurdly (but justifiably) specific skillset required you need to make sure people want to stay.

item.title: Re: Last note
item.content:encoded: It's a bit hand-holdy but I feel it fits with Foundation protocol. It's an alarming trend that must be made aware to all that read the file and who are assigned to it, so it can stay if there's no real need to get rid of it. It also helps people who just skim the article and don't look at the dates.

item.title: Re: Last note
item.content:encoded: It seems unnecessary, but it isn't hurting anything either.

item.title: Last note
item.content:encoded: A few people have been saying that the last note in this SCP is spelling things out too much. Should I go ahead and erase it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Also, it seems that if they learned about Site 19 from chatter between the MTF team, they might try redirecting 2629 to another location in the same way…"

There's a tale in that somewhere…

item.title: Re: Jr. Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Actually, it looks like Shaggy was the one who fixed that issue.

item.title: Re: Jr. Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Understood, I re-edited an edit I made before the Site-19 Fiasco, involving amnesiacs and amnestics

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with TheLordSavant, I think the last note overeggs the implication of the timestamps; the timestamps had me saying 'oh shit' long before I hit the last note. Happily upvoted, though, this is a light SCP done absolutely right.

item.title: Jr. Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
That is outside the bounds of the edits a person can make. I've reverted them. Try to keep your edits to just fixing spelling and grammar mistakes.

E. The author provided reasoning for why Site 19 was used here in the discussion page. And I feel it's better with the inclusion of 19.

item.title: Apologies
item.content:encoded: Look, I had to. Site-19 is so cannon, I blocked out all Site-19's you can revert it if you want though I don't advise it, it's better this way

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Keter Paintball is actually good Keter, who knew? I like that right now, this is mostly focused on fucking with the Foundation, but there's that sense of, they changed things up once, they could change it again. But yeah, nice to see a Keter that ain't gonna kill nobody dead. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just letting you know, since you have made 3 successful SCPs, you can now create an author page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the "why is this keter" to "lol, that's why, oh crabs" feeling this gives off, but I feel like the last note tells too much, especially because you've built up a great article of showing. I realized it when they started hitting site 19 and started doing so more often, and when it dawned on me, I thought, "Hot damn. This is an awesome level of subtle." But that feeling of figuring it out kinda got drained of its merit when I read the last note, spelling it out for me. That's me though. I think it works without the note, because it's well constructed overall. +1, because worldwide constantly-improving paintball scumbag ghost teenagers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly. It's perfectly in human nature, but is subtly done in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Containment failed due to MTF-Iota-17's inability to get good

I was wondering why this was keter before getting to the bit about paintball ghosts invading site 19.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definition: Scrub

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great, but one clarification:

Researcher ███ ██████ suffers significant eye trauma after being struck in the eye by a paintball.

So SCP-2629 is okay with breaking paintball rules, but throws a fit if the Greendale Humans1 do? That's just so perfectly jerk-ish. It goes a long way to make 2629 unsympathetic.

Footnotes
1. also great

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this, but I'm not sure why it would be the Ethics Committee denying the requests to neutralise it. Also, it seems that if they learned about Site 19 from chatter between the MTF team, they might try redirecting 2629 to another location in the same way, rather than just accepting that ghosts are going to trash the place periodically.

Other than that though, good stuff!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I used Site-19 for a number of reasons. Since it's such an important site it's much harder for the Foundation to just move. (And also it makes it that much funnier when it gets trashed).

As for how SCP-2629 knew about Site-19 when it wasn't in Poland, that has to do with the requirements for MTF-Iota-17. Since the skills required to join were so esoteric, the Polish branch had to get help from the wider Foundation. Most of the Task Force has probably passed through Site-19 at one point.

I see you also fixed some of my grammar. Nice job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahahaha this is a fun one

My only complaint is the use of Site-19 (it's such a big and important site - and I don't think it's in Poland?), and why didn't they just move out to another site?

I like how the time between losses got progressively shorter as the years went on, a good subtle touch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always enjoy your SCPs, Rockyred, and this one is no exception. I always love the "Oh… Oh no" feeling I get from SCPs, and this one does it perfectly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay…Third SCP, first Keter item. I'm a bit nervous, but that's always the case.
